signature: __init__(self, adata, layer_key = None, model = 'danb', latent_obsm_key = None, distances_obsp_key = None, tree = None, umi_counts_obs_key = None)
description: Initialize a Hotspot object for analysis  Either `latent` or `tree` or `distances` is required.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Count matrix (shape is cells by genes)

params.name: layer_key
params.desc: Key in adata.layers with count data, uses adata.X if None.

params.name: model
params.desc: Specifies the null model to use for gene expression. Valid choices are:  - 'danb': Depth-Adjusted Negative Binomial - 'bernoulli': Models probability of detection - 'normal': Depth-Adjusted Normal - 'none': Assumes data has been pre-standardized

params.name: latent_obsm_key
params.desc: Latent space encoding cell-cell similarities with euclidean distances.  Shape is (cells x dims). Input is key in adata.obsm

params.name: distances_obsp_key
params.desc: Distances encoding cell-cell similarities directly Shape is (cells x cells). Input is key in adata.obsp

params.name: tree
params.desc: Root tree node.  Can be created using ete3.Tree

signature: legacy_init(cls, counts, model = 'danb', latent = None, distances = None, tree = None, umi_counts = None)
description: Initialize a Hotspot object for analysis using legacy method   Either `latent` or `tree` or `distances` is required.

params.name: counts
params.desc: Count matrix (shape is genes x cells)

params.name: model
params.desc: Specifies the null model to use for gene expression. Valid choices are: - 'danb': Depth-Adjusted Negative Binomial - 'bernoulli': Models probability of detection - 'normal': Depth-Adjusted Normal - 'none': Assumes data has been pre-standardized

params.name: latent
params.desc: Latent space encoding cell-cell similarities with euclidean distances.  Shape is (cells x dims)

params.name: distances
params.desc: Distances encoding cell-cell similarities directly Shape is (cells x cells)

params.name: tree
params.desc: Root tree node.  Can be created using ete3.Tree

params.name: umi_counts
params.desc: Total umi count per cell.  Used as a size factor. If omitted, the sum over genes in the counts matrix is used

misc:
--------
>>> gene_exp = pd.read_csv(path, index_col=0) # genes by cells
>>> latent = pd.read_csv(latent_path, index_col=0) # cells by dims
>>> hs = hotspot.Hotspot.legacy_init(gene_exp, model="normal", latent=latent)

signature: create_knn_graph(self, weighted_graph = False, n_neighbors = 30, neighborhood_factor = 3, approx_neighbors = True)
description: Create's the KNN graph and graph weights  The resulting matrices containing the neighbors and weights are stored in the object at `self.neighbors` and `self.weights`

params.name: weighted_graph
params.desc: Whether or not to create a weighted graph

params.name: n_neighbors
params.desc: Neighborhood size

params.name: neighborhood_factor
params.desc: Used when creating a weighted graph.  Sets how quickly weights decay relative to the distances within the neighborhood.  The weight for a cell with a distance d will decay as exp(-d/D) where D is the distance to the `n_neighbors`/`neighborhood_factor`-th neighbor.

description: Perform feature selection using local autocorrelation  In addition to returning output, this also stores the output in `self.results`.  Alias for `self.compute_autocorrelations`

params.name: jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs to run

params.name: return
params.desc: A dataframe with four columns:  - C: Scaled -1:1 autocorrelation coeficients - Z: Z-score for autocorrelation - Pval:  P-values computed from Z-scores - FDR:  Q-values using the Benjamini-Hochberg procedure  Gene ids are in the index

description: Perform feature selection using local autocorrelation  In addition to returning output, this also stores the output in `self.results`

params.name: jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs to run

params.name: return
params.desc: A dataframe with four columns:  - C: Scaled -1:1 autocorrelation coeficients - Z: Z-score for autocorrelation - Pval:  P-values computed from Z-scores - FDR:  Q-values using the Benjamini-Hochberg procedure  Gene ids are in the index

description: Define gene-gene relationships with pair-wise local correlations  In addition to returning output, this method stores its result in `self.local_correlation_z`

params.name: genes
params.desc: gene identifies to compute local correlations on should be a smaller subset of all genes

params.name: jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs to run

params.name: return
params.desc: local correlation Z-scores between genes shape is genes x genes

description: Groups genes into modules  In addition to being returned, the results of this method are retained in the object at `self.modules`.  Additionally, the linkage matrix (in the same form as that of scipy.cluster.hierarchy.linkage) is saved in `self.linkage` for plotting or manual clustering.

params.name: min_gene_threshold
params.desc: Controls how small modules can be.  Increase if there are too many modules being formed.  Decrease if substructre is not being captured

params.name: core_only
params.desc: Whether or not to assign ambiguous genes to a module or leave unassigned

params.name: fdr_threshold
params.desc: Correlation theshold at which to stop assigning genes to modules

params.name: return
params.desc: Maps gene to module number.  Unassigned genes are indicated with -1

description: Calculate Module Scores  In addition to returning its result, this method stores its output in the object at `self.module_scores`

params.name: return
params.desc: Scores for each module for each gene Dimensions are genes x modules

signature: plot_local_correlations(self, mod_cmap = 'tab10', vmin = -8, vmax = 8, z_cmap = 'RdBu_r', yticklabels = False)
description: Plots a clustergrid of the local correlation values

params.name: mod_cmap
params.type: valid matplotlib colormap str or object
params.desc: discrete colormap for module assignments on the left side

params.name: vmin
params.desc: minimum value for colorscale for Z-scores

params.name: vmax
params.desc: maximum value for colorscale for Z-scores

params.name: z_cmap
params.type: valid matplotlib colormap str or object
params.desc: continuous colormap for correlation Z-scores
